title: EDHOC: TH_4 computation mismatch between initiator and responder
description:
CRITICAL PROTOCOL BUG: TH_4 (transcript hash 4) is computed differently between initiator and responder, causing OSCORE key derivation to produce different keys.

Initiator (line 711): compute_th(ctx->th_4, ctx->th_3, 32, plaintext_3, pt3_len, ctx->ed_pubkey, 32)
Computes: H(CBOR(TH_3) || CBOR(PLAINTEXT_3) || CBOR(CRED_I))

Responder (lines 1122-1129):
memcpy(th4_input + th4_len, ctx->th_3, 32); memcpy(th4_input + th4_len, msg3, msg3_len); sha256_hash(...)
Computes: H(TH_3 || CIPHERTEXT_3) - raw concat, no CBOR, uses ciphertext not plaintext, missing CRED_I

Per RFC 9528 Section 4.2.2: TH_4 = H(TH_3, PLAINTEXT_3, CRED_I) for both parties.

Impact: Initiator and responder will derive different OSCORE master secrets, breaking E2E encryption.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:1122-1129
close_reason: Fixed: Responder TH_4 computation now uses compute_th() with PLAINTEXT_3 and CRED_I instead of raw memcpy with CIPHERTEXT_3. Both parties now compute TH_4 = H(CBOR(TH_3) || CBOR(PLAINTEXT_3) || CBOR(CRED_I)) per RFC 9528 Section 4.2.2.
